items.title: One training job → one tracked run
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  scriptNode["training script"]
  runNode["run ID assigned"]
  paramsNode["log params / config"]
  metricsNode["log metrics per step"]
  artifactsNode["log artifacts"]
  storeNode["tracking backend"]
  scriptNode --> runNode
  runNode --> paramsNode
  runNode --> metricsNode
  runNode --> artifactsNode
  paramsNode --> storeNode
  metricsNode --> storeNode
  artifactsNode --> storeNode
items.caption: MLflow and W&B wrap each execution in a run that captures params, time-series metrics, and files in one record.

items.title: Comparing multiple runs
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  runANode["run A: lr=0.01, val_f1=0.82"]
  runBNode["run B: lr=0.05, val_f1=0.88"]
  runCNode["run C: lr=0.10, val_f1=0.85"]
  filterNode["filter: tag candidate, same data_version"]
  compareNode["UI table / parallel coords / API search"]
  pickNode["select best val_f1 run"]
  runANode --> filterNode
  runBNode --> filterNode
  runCNode --> filterNode
  filterNode --> compareNode
  compareNode --> pickNode
items.caption: Fair comparison filters runs on shared conditions (data version, split) then aligns params and metrics side by side.

items.title: MLflow vs W&B placement in workflow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  trainNode["train model"]
  mlflowNode["MLflow Tracking: params, metrics, artifacts"]
  wandbNode["W&B: live dashboards, sweeps"]
  registryNode["model registry / promotion"]
  trainNode --> mlflowNode
  trainNode --> wandbNode
  mlflowNode --> registryNode
  wandbNode --> registryNode
items.caption: Both tools log runs during training; MLflow often feeds registry workflows while W&B emphasizes team visibility — many teams use one or both.
